finding: Slovak turnover contraction deepened as labour costs outpaced euro area peers
bullets: Slovak labour costs rose for a net 84% of firms (n=229), up 21.78 pp from the prior wave's net 62%, widening the gap above the euro area's net 63% to over 20 pp — putting further pressure on margins.

A net 26% of Slovak firms (n=229) reported falling turnover, deteriorating 11.60 pp from the prior wave's net 15%, contrasting sharply with the euro area's near-flat net 1% — signalling a sharper demand downturn.
Slovak profits declined for a net 40% of firms (n=229), worsening 17.37 pp from the prior wave's net 22% — more than double the euro area's net 17% contraction pace.
chart_subtitle: A net 26% of Slovak firms reported falling turnover.
